statement.focus_question: What if instead we ask for $G$ to have chromatic number $\aleph_1$?
statement.normalized: Is there a graph $G$ with vertex set $\omega_2^2$ and chromatic number $\aleph_2$ such that every subgraph whose vertices have a lesser type has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$? What if instead we ask for $G$ to have chromatic number $\aleph_1$?
statement.original:
Is there a graph $G$ with vertex set $\omega_2^2$ and chromatic number $\aleph_2$ such that every subgraph whose vertices have a lesser type has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$?

What if instead we ask for $G$ to have chromatic number $\aleph_1$?
